_comment: Pins the SIGNED-ORIGIN contract end-to-end: the frame JSON on disk holds
true negative x/y, the analyzer deserializes them into `Region`'s i32
origin, and every expectation below flips if those coordinates are
clamped to 0 the way the old u32 origin forced producers to do.
With a clamped origin the layout analyzer emits FIVE overlaps here (every
off-screen node collapses onto the viewport top-left); with the true
signed origin it emits exactly ONE — the sticky header that genuinely
straddles y=0 and covers the page title. Hence count_max: 1.
no_overlap[skip-link, drawer]        fail -> pass  (a11y node is really off-canvas)
no_overlap[sticky-header, page-title] fail -> fail (a REAL overlap across y=0,
proving negative y is used, not just tolerated)
aligned_vertically[drawer, sticky-header] pass -> fail (x=-320 vs x=0 is a
